description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its top surface, creating a slatted or grated appearance. The grooves are deep and extend the full width of the block, giving it a distinctive striped pattern when viewed from above. The stone material is mottled with shades of gray, black, and occasional flecks of lighter color, suggesting a composite or aggregate stone. The sides of the block are solid and smooth, contrasting with the textured, grooved top. The block is thick and robust, likely designed to serve as a heavy-duty barrier or obstacle in an urban environment. Its overall form is geometric and utilitarian, suitable for use in public infrastructure or as a protective element.
description: A thick, rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves across the top, featuring a mottled gray and black stone texture. The sides are solid and smooth, emphasizing its robust and utilitarian design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the grooved top surface with the grooves appearing as horizontal lines and the solid, smooth front face.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, which run vertically from this perspective, and the solid side face.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, with the grooved top surface visible and the solid back face.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the vertical grooves and the solid side face, mirroring view 1.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or street drainage area
attribute_installation_method: embedded in concrete or set flush with pavement
attribute_weathering_pattern: No significant weathering is visible; the surface appears clean and well-maintained, with no moss, chips, or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: set flush with adjacent pavement or ground surface
